title: Constitution of the MUST Writers Guild

articles.title: Name
content: The name of this club shall be the MUST Writers Guild, abbreviated as ‘MWG,’ hereinafter referred to as “the Guild.”

articles.title: Mission
content: To promote a vibrant culture of creative writing by providing a platform for students to express themselves, grow as writers, and connect with a like-minded community.

articles.title: Objectives
content: 1. Publish student works through the Guild's platforms, including digital anthologies, newsletters, and online blogs.
2. Organize workshops, writing competitions, literary events, and social gatherings to make participation fun and engaging.
3. Provide peer review, mentorship opportunities, and skill-building sessions to support member development.
4. Foster a culture of reading and literary appreciation on campus through book clubs, author discussions, and reading challenges.
5. Collaborate with other literary, academic, and student bodies within and outside the University to expand the Guild's footprint and visibility.
6. Encourage active member contributions through mandatory participation in events, projects, or committees to ensure ongoing engagement and impact.

articles.title: Values
content: 1. Creativity: We encourage original expression and literary exploration.
2. Integrity: We value honesty and respect for intellectual property.
3. Inclusivity: We embrace diverse perspectives and foster an environment where all feel welcome.
4. Collaboration: We promote constructive feedback, shared learning, and collective progress.
5. Engagement: We prioritize fun, interactive activities that strengthen community bonds and active participation.

articles.title: Membership
content: Section 1: Eligibility and Criteria
1. Membership is open to all registered students of the Malawi University of Science and Technology (MUST), regardless of field of study. Alumni inclusive.
2. To become a member, applicants must meet the following criteria:
a. Demonstrate a genuine interest in creative writing, evidenced by submitting a short writing sample (e.g., poem, story excerpt, or essay) via the application form.

b. Commit to active participation, including attending at least three meetings or events per semester and contributing to at least one Guild project (e.g., event organization, content creation, or peer review).
c. Agree to uphold the Guild's values, code of conduct, and objectives.
d. There shall be no discrimination on the basis of race, gender, religion, disability, or any other status.
Section 2: Application Process
1. Applications for membership shall be accepted during specific windows at the beginning of each semester (e.g., the first two weeks of the semester) or as announced by the Executive Committee.

2. Applications must be submitted through an official Google Form provided by the Guild, which will collect necessary details such as student ID, contact information, writing sample, application fee, and a statement of interest.

3. The Executive Committee shall review applications within one week of the window closing and notify applicants of their status. Successful applicants may be required to pay a registration fee in an amount decided by the Executive Committee.
4. Membership is probationary for the first semester, during which new members must fulfill attendance and contribution requirements to gain full status.
Section 3: Rights and Responsibilities
1. Members in good standing shall have voting rights, the ability to run for office, and access to all Guild resources and events.

2. Members must maintain active involvement; failure to meet attendance or contribution requirements may result in review by the Executive Committee, potentially leading to suspension or revocation of membership.
3. All members are required to pay a membership fee amounting to K5,000 each semester.

articles.title: Executive Committee
content: Section 1: Composition
The Executive Committee shall consist of the Chairperson, Vice Chairperson, Secretary, Treasurer, and Publicity and Events Coordinators.
Section 2: Duties

1. Chairperson: a. Provide overall leadership and direction. b. Chair meetings and represent the Guild in official matters. c. Ensure the Guild is registered with respective university bodies and complies with all policies.
2. Vice Chairperson: a. Assist the Chairperson and act in their absence. b. Oversee membership recruitment, retention, and internal affairs, including monitoring active participation.
3. Secretary: a. Maintain records, minutes of meetings, and all communications. b. Manage membership databases and application processes.
4. Treasurer: a. Manage finances, keep accurate records, and prepare financial reports. b. Oversee fundraising efforts and budget allocation for engaging events.

5. Publicity and Events Coordinator: a. Promote the Guild through social media, announcements, and campus outreach to strengthen visibility. b. Plan and organize workshops, readings, competitions, and fun social events (e.g., writing jams, themed nights) to enhance engagement.
Section 3: Elections
1. Elections for the Executive Committee shall be held annually at the Annual General Meeting (AGM), towards the end of the first semester of each academic year.
2. All members in good standing are eligible to vote and stand for office.
3. The position of Chairperson shall only be held by continuing students who have completed at least one full academic year at MUST, unless otherwise approved by the Executive Committee.
4. A simple majority vote is required to elect each officer. Voting shall be conducted via secret ballot or secure electronic means.
5. An individual may serve in the same Executive Committee position for a maximum of two consecutive terms. Such a member may seek election to a different position for an additional term.
6. Nominations must be submitted at least one week prior to the AGM to allow for candidate statements and member review.
Section 4: Vacancy and Replacement

1. In the event that an Executive Committee member resigns, is withdrawn from the university, is incapacitated, dies, or is removed for misconduct, the position shall be declared vacant by the remaining Executive Committee.
2. The Executive Committee shall, within two weeks, appoint a replacement from among eligible members, subject to ratification by a majority vote at the next general meeting.
3. If the position of Chairperson becomes vacant, the Vice Chairperson shall assume the role of Acting Chairperson until a new Chairperson is elected or appointed.

4. A by-election shall be held if the remaining term exceeds three months and the position is deemed critical to the Guild's operations. By-elections must follow the same procedures as annual elections.

articles.title: Meetings
content: 1. General meetings shall be held at least twice a month during the academic semester to discuss activities, share writings, and plan events.
2. Executive Committee meetings shall be held monthly or as needed.
3. Quorum for general meetings shall be one-third of the registered members. For Executive Committee meetings, quorum shall be a majority of the committee.
4. Notice for all meetings shall be provided at least 48 hours in advance via email, social media, or other official channels.
5. Special meetings may be called by the Chairperson or upon petition by at least 10% of members.

articles.title: Finances
content: 1. The Guild shall be funded through university grants, donations, fundraising activities, sponsorships, and member registration fees (if applicable).
2. All financial transactions must be documented, authorized by the Treasurer and Chairperson, and adhere to university financial policies.
3. An annual financial report shall be presented to members at the AGM, with quarterly updates provided at general meetings for transparency.
4. The Executive Committee may appoint an independent auditor (e.g., a university staff member) annually to review finances.
5. Funds shall prioritize engaging activities, such as prizes for competitions, guest speakers, and publication costs.

articles.title: Amendments
content: 1. Any member may propose an amendment to the Constitution by submitting it in writing to the Executive Committee at least two weeks prior to a general meeting.
2. Amendments must be approved by a two-thirds majority of members present and voting at a general meeting, provided quorum is met.
3. Notice of proposed amendments must be distributed to all members at least one week in advance.
4. Approved amendments shall take effect immediately unless otherwise stated.

articles.title: Code of Conduct
content: 1. All members are expected to uphold respectful communication, promote inclusivity, avoid plagiarism or any form of academic dishonesty, and actively contribute to Guild activities.
2. Members shall respect confidentiality in peer reviews and foster a positive, fun environment during events.
3. Violations may include harassment, repeated non-participation, or unethical behavior, and shall result in disciplinary action subject to review by the Executive Committee.

articles.title: Disciplinary Procedures
content: 1. A disciplinary committee comprising three impartial members (including at least one non-Executive member) shall handle grievances or misconduct.
2. A fair hearing must be granted, including written notice of charges, opportunity to respond, and access to evidence.
3. Disciplinary measures may include warnings, probation, suspension, or expulsion from the Guild. Decisions may be appealed to the full membership at a general meeting.
4. All procedures shall comply with university policies on student conduct.

articles.title: Committees and Sub-Groups
content: 1. The Guild may establish standing or ad hoc committees (e.g., Publications Committee, Events Committee, Outreach Committee) to manage specific functions.
2. Each committee shall be chaired by a member appointed by the Executive Committee and shall report regularly to the Executive Committee.
3. Committees must operate within the Constitution’s framework and promote active member involvement.

articles.title: Publications Policy
content: 1. All works submitted for publication under the Guild’s name must be original and free from plagiarism.
2. The Guild retains the right to edit submissions for clarity and style, ensuring authors are consulted and appropriately credited.
3. No work shall be published without the author’s explicit written consent.
4. Publications shall aim to showcase diverse voices and include fun elements like themed issues or collaborative pieces.

articles.title: Partnerships and Collaborations
content: 1. The Guild may collaborate with student bodies, NGOs, academic departments, or literary organizations for mutual benefit, such as joint events or workshops.
2. All partnerships must be approved by a majority vote of the Executive Committee and documented in writing.
3. Partnerships shall align with the Guild's objectives and enhance campus footprint through co-branded activities.

articles.title: Alumni Involvement
content: 1. Graduated members may be recognized as Alumni Members upon request.
2. Alumni Members may not vote or hold office but may be invited to mentor current members, participate in select events and projects, or serve as guest speakers.
3. Alumni Members may provide financial or advisory support toward Guild activities.

articles.title: Annual General Meeting (AGM)
content: 1. An Annual General Meeting shall be held towards the end of each academic year.
2. During the AGM, annual reports (including financial and activity summaries) shall be presented, and any constitutional amendments discussed and ratified.
3. Notice for the AGM shall be provided at least two weeks in advance, with an agenda distributed to all members.

articles.title: Patron
content: 1. The Guild shall have a Patron who is a member of the University staff, approved by the Executive Committee.
2. The Patron shall provide mentorship, guidance, and support to the Guild and may attend key meetings and events as an honorary advisor.
3. The Patron shall not be involved in the day-to-day administration of the Guild and shall not have voting rights.

articles.title: Dissolution
content: 1. The Guild may be dissolved by a two-thirds majority vote of all registered members at a special meeting called for this purpose.
2. Upon dissolution, any remaining assets shall be transferred to a related student organization or university office, subject to university policies.
3. Notice for a dissolution vote must be provided at least four weeks in advance.
